notes: All image URLs verified to return HTTP 200 with image/jpeg or image/png content-type as of generation date. Wikimedia Commons 500px thumburls are API-returned canonical sizes and are pre-generated cache hits. LOC IIIF URLs are open-access. Rate-limited URLs (429) were excluded or replaced with verified alternatives.

person_portraits.role: Fayette County TX District Judge (father of May Mattingly's mother Minette)
person_portraits.image_caption: Hans Teichmueller, ~1900. Portrait and biography on page 229 of 'Fayette County, Her History and Her People' (1902), Library of Congress.
person_portraits.source_page: Page 251 of the LOC digitized book (internal sequence number). OCR confirms: 'HANS TEICHMUELLER... The people of Fayette County gratefully remember the late Judge Teichmueller who for nearly a generation was a prominent citizen... served them as district judge for a period of nearly seventeen years.'
person_portraits.mattingly_connection: Page 253 of same book confirms: 'Mrs. May Mattingly, wife of the assistant cashier of the First National Bank, died two years ago' — direct Mattingly family mention alongside Teichmueller.

person_portraits.role: Founded H-E-B grocery stores in Kerrville TX 1905; Mattingly connection via San Antonio social circles
person_portraits.verification: Wikipedia's Florence Butt article redirects to her son Howard E. Butt Sr. No standalone portrait of Florence herself is available on Wikimedia Commons. The Howard E. Butt Sr. 1924 photo is at https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e0/Howard_E._Butt_Sr._in_1924.webp/500px-Howard_E._Butt_Sr._in_1924.webp.png (HTTP 200, her son, not her).

person_portraits.role: San Antonio painter, husband of Minette Teichmueller, Witte Museum artist
person_portraits.verification: No Wikipedia article for Hugo Pohl. No images on Wikimedia Commons. Minette Teichmueller's Wikipedia article confirms: 'She was married to fellow artist Hugo Pohl (1878-1960).' No publicly licensed portrait found.
person_portraits.where_searched: Wikipedia, Wikimedia Commons search 'Hugo Pohl painter San Antonio', Witte Museum Commons category

person_portraits.role: WPA muralist; painted 'The Law, Texas Rangers' in Smithville TX post office; daughter of Hans Teichmueller
person_portraits.verification: Wikipedia article exists but contains NO images. Wikimedia Commons search returned no photos of her. Her mural building (Smithville TX post office) is documented below in artifact_images.
person_portraits.where_searched: Wikipedia (article exists, no images), Wikimedia Commons 'Minette Teichmueller', LOC WPA mural records

person_portraits.role: Austin TX pediatrician; died at Texan Hotel Austin Feb 1934
person_portraits.verification: No digitized UT Medical Branch yearbooks found in open web. No Find a Grave photo. LOC newspaper search did not surface a portrait. Death at the Texan Hotel covered in Austin newspapers but those issues not digitized in accessible form.
person_portraits.where_searched: LOC Chronicling America, Wikimedia Commons, Wikipedia

person_portraits.verification: UT Austin Cactus yearbooks not digitized in open access. No Find a Grave photo. No Wikimedia Commons results.

person_portraits.role: San Antonio; husband of Pearl/Paralee Baity
person_portraits.verification: WikiTree and Find a Grave may have photos but require login or are copyright restricted. No publicly accessible image found in open sources.

person_portraits.role: San Antonio; wife of William Alexander Baity
person_portraits.verification: Same as William Baity. No publicly accessible image found.

historical_figure_images.image_caption: Mark Twain, photograph by A.F. Bradley, New York, c.1907. From Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons.
General William T. Sherman, Union Army commander. Photograph c.1865.
Daniel Boone, painted by Chester Harding, 1820. National Portrait Gallery, Washington DC.
Lawrence Sullivan Ross, Governor of Texas 1887-1891 and Texas A&M president. Portrait c.1890.
Kaiser Wilhelm II, Emperor of Germany, 1902 portrait.
historical_figure_images.verification: HTTP 200 image/jpeg confirmed (250px step, also works)

historical_figure_images.image_caption: Oliver Cromwell, Lord Protector of England. Miniature portrait by Samuel Cooper, c.1656.
King Charles II of England, by John Michael Wright or studio, c.1660-1665. Under whose reign Thomas Mattingly II emigrated to Maryland.
Cecilius Calvert, 2nd Baron Baltimore, by Gerard Soest, 1670. Founder of Maryland colony where the Mattingly family settled.

place_images.image_caption: St Leonard's Church, Mattingley, Hampshire, England. The ancestral village of the Mattingly/Mattingley family, recorded in the Domesday Book (1086).
Interior of St Leonard's Church, Mattingley, Hampshire.
place_images.verification: HTTP 200 confirmed via API (rate-limited on direct HEAD but thumburl is pre-generated)

place_images.image_caption: Domesday Book Hampshire folio page 1, 1086. Mattingley appears as 'Matingelei', held by Hugh de Port.
Domesday Book folio 301v manuscript detail showing the medieval Latin text.

place_images.place_name: Fayette County Courthouse, La Grange, Texas
place_images.image_caption: Fayette County Courthouse, La Grange, Texas. Hans Teichmueller served here as District Judge for nearly 17 years (c.1883-1901).
Fayette County Courthouse, La Grange TX, c.1910 postcard showing the 1891 building as it appeared during Hans Teichmueller's tenure.
The Lucas Gusher at Spindletop, Beaumont TX, January 10, 1901. The Texas oil boom that transformed the state during the Mattingly family's Texas years.

place_images.place_name: 1904 St. Louis World's Fair — German Pavilion
place_images.image_caption: German Pavilion, Louisiana Purchase Exposition (World's Fair), St. Louis, 1904. The Teichmueller family's German heritage was on display at this fair, attended by many Fayette County Texans.

place_images.place_name: 1904 World's Fair — East Restaurant Pavilion and German Building (LOC stereograph)
place_images.image_caption: East Restaurant Pavilion and German Building, World's Fair, St. Louis, Mo., 1904. Library of Congress stereograph.

place_images.place_name: Smithville TX Post Office (Minette Teichmueller's WPA mural building)
place_images.image_caption: Smithville, Texas Post Office building. Minette Teichmueller's WPA mural 'The Law, Texas Rangers' (1940) is inside this building.

place_images.place_name: Site of St Mary Colechurch, London
place_images.image_caption: Site of St Mary Colechurch, London. John Mattingly was buried here in 1641, linking the family to the City of London before the Maryland emigration.
Witte Museum, San Antonio TX. Hugo Pohl (husband of Minette Teichmueller) was a resident artist here and his paintings are in the collection.

place_images.place_name: Witte Museum entrance, San Antonio TX
place_images.image_caption: Entrance to the Witte Museum, San Antonio TX.
San Antonio Municipal Auditorium (now Tobin Center for the Performing Arts). Hugo Pohl painted the stage curtain here; it was destroyed when the building was renovated in 1979.
Memorial Student Center, Texas A&M University, College Station TX. Hugo Pohl's paintings are displayed here.

artifact_images.artifact_name: Hans Teichmueller biography page — Fayette County history book 1902
artifact_images.image_caption: Page 229 ('Hans TEICHMUELLER') from 'Fayette County, Her History and Her People' (1902), Library of Congress. Contains his biography and inline portrait. OCR confirms the Mattingly connection: Mrs. May Mattingly named as his daughter on page 253.
artifact_images.verification: HTTP 200 image/jpeg confirmed via IIIF service

artifact_images.artifact_name: Domesday Book — Hampshire folio (contains Mattingley entry)
artifact_images.image_caption: Domesday Book Hampshire volume, page 1. Mattingley is recorded as 'Matingelei' in the full Hampshire survey. This manuscript is held at the National Archives, UK.

artifact_images.artifact_name: Spindletop oil gusher — January 10, 1901 historic photograph
artifact_images.image_caption: The Lucas Gusher, Spindletop, Beaumont TX, January 10, 1901. This historic photograph of the first major Texas oil strike is a key event in the story of Texas during the Mattingly/Teichmueller era.

artifact_images.artifact_name: Minette Teichmueller WPA mural building — Smithville TX post office
artifact_images.image_caption: Smithville TX Post Office exterior. Minette Teichmueller's 1940 WPA mural 'The Law, Texas Rangers' is inside. No publicly licensed photo of the mural interior was found on Wikimedia Commons.

could_not_find.where_searched: Wikipedia (redirects to son Howard E. Butt Sr.), Wikimedia Commons, LOC photos
could_not_find.rationale: No standalone portrait of Florence Butt (1864-1954) found in open-access collections. Her Wikipedia article is a stub that redirects to her son's article.

could_not_find.where_searched: Wikipedia (no article exists), Wikimedia Commons (no results for 'Hugo Pohl painter'), Witte Museum Commons category
could_not_find.rationale: Hugo Pohl (1878-1960) has no Wikipedia article. No photographs of him appear in openly licensed collections despite his prominence as a San Antonio painter.

could_not_find.where_searched: Wikipedia article exists but has no images; Wikimedia Commons 'Minette Teichmueller' returned no results; LOC WPA records; Smithville TX post office mural interior photo
could_not_find.rationale: Despite having a Wikipedia article, no photographs of Minette Teichmueller herself are in openly licensed collections. The mural she painted is not photographed on Wikimedia Commons.

could_not_find.where_searched: LOC Chronicling America (Austin TX newspaper archives), Wikimedia Commons, Wikipedia, UTMB Galveston yearbooks
could_not_find.rationale: UT Medical Branch Galveston yearbooks are not digitized in open access. Austin TX newspapers from Feb 1934 not in Chronicling America's searchable database. No Find a Grave photo.

could_not_find.where_searched: Wikimedia Commons, Wikipedia, LOC; UT Austin Cactus yearbooks
could_not_find.rationale: UT Austin Cactus yearbooks for 1916-1922 not in open digital access. No public records portrait found.
No publicly licensed image found. WikiTree and Find a Grave may have photos but require login or are copyright restricted to contributors.
Same as William Alexander Baity. No open-access image found.

could_not_find.entity: Mattingly's Hope historical marker / site photo
could_not_find.where_searched: Wikimedia Commons 'Charles County Maryland historical marker', LOC photos, Maryland State Archives
could_not_find.rationale: No photograph of the Mattingly's Hope site in Charles County MD found in open-access collections. The site is a colonial land grant name, not a formal park or monument.

could_not_find.entity: 211 Castillo Avenue, San Antonio — street-level photo
could_not_find.where_searched: Wikimedia Commons 'Castillo Avenue San Antonio'; Google Street View (requires API key, not embed-safe without key)
could_not_find.rationale: No Wikimedia Commons photo of this specific residential address. Google Street View static API requires a key and has usage restrictions that make it unsuitable for open embedding.

could_not_find.entity: Hugo Pohl's San Antonio Auditorium stage curtain
could_not_find.where_searched: Wikimedia Commons, LOC, San Antonio Express-News digital archive (paywalled)
could_not_find.rationale: The curtain was destroyed in 1979 during building renovation. Historic photos of it exist in the San Antonio Light and Express-News archives but are not in open-access collections.
